Story: As a new user I can create my account and profile so that I can start using the platform
Acceptance Criteria:
- User registration completes successfully
- Profile created with basic information
- Email verification sent and validated
- User assigned to primary context automatically

Story: As a new user I can create my first context to organize my information so that I have a space to begin adding content
Acceptance Criteria:
- Context created with title and description
- Context visibility set to private by default
- User becomes context owner
- Primary context designated

Story: As a new user I can browse available context templates so that I can leverage best practices without starting from scratch
Acceptance Criteria:
- Template library displayed with categories
- Preview template structure and purpose
- Search templates by use case
- Template metadata shown (creator, usage count, ratings)

Story: As a new user I can apply a context template so that I can quickly set up my space with proven structures
Acceptance Criteria:
- Select template from library
- Template applied to context within 5 seconds
- Nodes and structure created correctly
- User guided through customization options

Story: As a user I can create a default node with title and markdown body so that I can capture basic information
Acceptance Criteria:
- POST /api/v1/nodes returns 201 + node_id
- Node appears in DB and primary context
- Auto-generate unique slug
- Assign to primary context
- Choose existence type (Essential, Purposed, Functional, Temporary)
- Set node type (default, document, condition, start, end, custom)

Story: As a user I can create a document node composed of ordered block nodes so that I can structure rich content
Acceptance Criteria:
- Document = ordered collection of block nodes
- Each paragraph/section = potential block node
- Rich-text editing with markdown
- Inline formatting and media
- Document stores ordered list of block IDs
- Renders with <200ms for 100 blocks

Story: As a user I can extract a paragraph into a standalone node preserving backlinks so that I can create modular reusable content
Acceptance Criteria:
- Select block and "Extract to Node"
- Creates new node with backlink automatically
- Original replaced with reference/embed
- Maintains document flow
- Operation returns new node_id
- Backlinks tracked automatically

Story: As a user I can add specific type data elements to nodes so that I can capture structured information
Acceptance Criteria:
- Add datatypes, interfaces, attributes, formulas
- Connect to slots
- Data elements are version-protected
- Support multiple data element types per node
- Validation on data element creation

Story: As a user the system detects duplicate nodes within same context and prompts me to merge so that I avoid information redundancy
Acceptance Criteria:
- Detect duplicates within same context
- Show similarity scores
- API returns 409 DUPLICATE with merge suggestions
- Cross-context duplicates = suggest relationships (not error)
- Bulk deduplication wizard available

Story: As a user I can connect two nodes with a wire and select relation type so that I can express relationships between information
Acceptance Criteria:
- Connect two nodes with wire
- POST /api/v1/wires creates edge
- Select relation type via attribute
- Set direction (one_way, two_way)
- Add wire metadata/description
- Visual wire creation in VizView
- VizView render shows the connection

Story: As a user hovering a wire shows tooltip with attribute summary so that I can quickly understand relationships
Acceptance Criteria:
- Wire states: Selected / Unselected / Highlighted Selected / Highlighted Unselected
- Hover shows metadata tooltip within 150ms
- Tooltip shows title + relation_type
- State machine per wire (frontend)
- Animation transitions between states (<100ms)

Story: As a user I can select multiple nodes and wire them to a target so that I can efficiently create relationship patterns
Acceptance Criteria:
- Select multiple nodes and wire to target
- Family/system relation propagation
- Wire templates for common patterns
- Undo/redo wire operations
- Bulk wire creation supports 100+ operations
- Preview before applying

Story: As a user I can follow wires to explore connected nodes so that I can discover related information
Acceptance Criteria:
- Follow wires to connected nodes
- View all incoming wires (backlinks)
- View all outgoing wires
- Filter wires by relation type
- Trace connection paths
- No orphaned wires (cascade deletion)

Story: As a user I can create connections using GUI interactions so that I can visually wire relationships
Acceptance Criteria:
- Visual wiring interface
- Drag-and-drop wire creation
- Visual feedback during wiring
- Connection preview before creation
- Support for multiple wire types

Story: As a professional user I can create various types of attributes so that I can grow the structure and depth of information
Acceptance Criteria:
- Define attribute with title and properties
- Associate with multiple nodes
- Set display type (label, button, tag, block, data_element)
- Configure existence information per context
- POST /api/v1/attributes persists object
- Existence info validated

Story: As a user I can create attributes for the attribute including a structure so that I can grow the depth and make it more fruitful
Acceptance Criteria:
- Define existed_node (attribute refers to)
- Define existence_node (container)
- Set existence_type (origin, clone, prefab, temporary)
- Set relation_type (has_a, depends_on, part_of, etc.)
- Link to specific context
- Existence information tracked per context

Story: As a user the system suggests relevant attributes based on keywords so that I can quickly enrich my information
Acceptance Criteria:
- System suggests attributes based on keywords
- Shared context = suggest common attributes
- Confidence scoring
- Accept/reject suggestions
- GET /api/v1/attributes/suggest returns ranked list
- Suggestions return within 500ms
- Learn from user choices

Story: As a user I can apply attribute to up to 100 nodes with propagation so that I can efficiently structure large information sets
Acceptance Criteria:
- Apply attribute to tree of nodes
- Propagate family/system relations
